text.title: Incident Response Blameless Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Incident Response Blameless Playbook` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The playbook MUST contain a published severity matrix (SEV1..SEV4) with concrete trigger criteria per row (customer-impact, data-loss surface, % degraded, duration). Reviewer hunch-severity is forbidden.

rationale.source: Google SRE Workbook ch. 9; PagerDuty Incident Response

rule.rationale: Hunch-severity collapses every incident into SEV1 or SEV3; pinned criteria stop the team from over-paging or under-paging.

rule.statement: The Incident Commander, Comms Lead, Scribe, Ops Lead, and Customer Liaison MUST be named per shift with a documented escalation path. Group handles are forbidden.

rationale.source: Atlassian Incident Handbook; Google SRE Managing Incidents

rule.rationale: Group-handle IC = no IC; named per-shift assignment makes the chain of command instant and unambiguous.

rule.statement: The playbook MUST ship internal-status / status-page / customer-email templates with placeholders (no free-prose comms during incidents).

rationale.source: PagerDuty Comms playbook; Mailchimp / Slack 2024 postmortems

rule.rationale: Comms drafted live during incidents introduce errors and delay; templates reduce cognitive load + cycle time.

rule.statement: Every SEV1 / SEV2 MUST trigger a blameless retro within 5 business days with a structured template (timeline / impact / contributing factors / action items / owners). Names are not censored, but blame language is.

rationale.source: Etsy Blameless Postmortems; Google SRE Postmortems

rule.rationale: Skipped retros cause repeat incidents; blame-laden retros suppress signal — the structured template surfaces both.

rule.statement: Retro action items MUST carry a single named owner, a due date, and a tracking ticket. Items without an owner or due date are auto-rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Orphan action items rot at 80%+ within 6 months; ownership + tracking flips completion to 60%+.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
